2605.12264.title: Reconstruction of Personally Identifiable Information from Supervised Finetuned Models
2605.12264.paper_summary: Supervised Finetuning (SFT) has become one of the primary methods for adapting a large language model (LLM) with extensive pre-trained knowledge to domain-specific, instruction-following tasks. SFT datasets, composed of instruction-response pairs, often include user-provided information that may contain sensitive data such as personally identifiable information (PII), raising privacy concerns. This paper studies the problem of PII reconstruction from SFT models for the first time. We construct multi-turn, user-centric Q&A datasets in sensitive domains, specifically medical and legal settings, that incorporate PII to enable realistic evaluation of leakage. Using these datasets, we evaluate the extent to which an adversary, with varying levels of knowledge about the fine-tuning dataset, can infer sensitive information about individuals whose data was used during SFT. In the reconstruction setting, we propose COVA, a novel decoding algorithm to reconstruct PII under prefix-based attacks, consistently outperforming existing extraction methods. Our results show that even partial attacker knowledge can significantly improve reconstruction success, while leakage varies substantially across PII types.
2605.12264.paper_summary_zh: 監督微調（SFT）已成為將擁有廣泛預訓練知識的大型語言模型（LLM）適應於特定領域、遵循指令任務的主要方法之一。SFT 數據集由指令-回應對組成，通常包括用戶提供的信息，這些信息可能包含敏感數據，如個人識別信息（PII），引發隱私問題。本文首次研究了從 SFT 模型重建 PII 的問題。我們構建了多輪用戶中心的問答數據集，專注於敏感領域，特別是醫療和法律環境，這些數據集包含 PII，以便能夠進行現實的洩漏評估。利用這些數據集，我們評估了具有不同知識水平的對手在多大程度上可以推斷出在 SFT 中使用的個體的敏感信息。在重建設置中，我們提出了 COVA，一種新穎的解碼算法，用於在基於前綴的攻擊下重建 PII，並始終優於現有的提取方法。我們的結果顯示，即使是部分攻擊者的知識也能顯著提高重建成功率，而洩漏在不同的 PII 類型之間變化顯著。
2605.12264.author: Sae Furukawa et.al.
